picks.game: Carolina Hurricanes vs Vegas Golden Knights
picks.rationale: Vegas holds a 2-1 series lead and benefits from home-ice advantage in Game 4. They have dominated recent head-to-head matchups, winning 4 of the last 5 against Carolina, and boast an impressive 8-2-0 record in their last 10 games, including a 6-1-0 mark in their last seven home games. The uncertainty surrounding Carolina's starting goaltender, with Frederik Andersen's recent struggles and 'maintenance day,' further tilts the advantage towards the Golden Knights. The -104 odds present solid value for the home team with momentum.

picks.game: Carolina Hurricanes vs Vegas Golden Knights
picks.rationale: The scoring trend in this series and recent head-to-head history heavily favors the Over. All three Stanley Cup Final games have surpassed 5.5 goals, with an average of 7.8 goals per game in the 2025-26 matchups. Both teams possess strong offensive capabilities, with Vegas boasting the top playoff offense and both teams finding success on the power play. Carolina's potential goaltending instability could also contribute to a higher-scoring contest, making the Over a high-confidence play.

picks.game: Carolina Hurricanes vs Vegas Golden Knights
picks.rationale: Despite the heavily juiced odds, this pick is highly confident given the tight nature of the Stanley Cup Final, with Vegas currently leading 2-1. The Golden Knights have won 4 of the last 5 head-to-head matchups against Carolina, and even in their losses, the games have been decided by a single goal. With strong home-ice advantage and a competitive series, it is very likely Vegas will either win outright or lose by only one goal, making the +1.5 spread a secure option.

picks.game: Carolina Hurricanes vs Vegas Golden Knights
picks.rationale: Despite dropping Game 3 in double overtime, Carolina remains a formidable team with a superior regular-season record of 53-22-7 and a strong motivation to even the series at 2-2. They have a proven ability to win on the road, with 8 victories in their last 10 away games before the recent loss. The Hurricanes' aggressive forecheck and offensive depth provide a strong foundation for a bounce-back performance, making their moneyline a high-confidence play for a pivotal Game 4.

picks.game: Carolina Hurricanes vs Vegas Golden Knights
picks.rationale: Despite trailing in the series, the Carolina Hurricanes possess a significantly superior regular-season record (53-22-7) and are highly motivated to avoid a 3-1 series deficit after an 'excruciating' Game 3 loss. They have demonstrated strong offensive capabilities, ranking 2nd in goals for during the regular season, and have a solid road record, winning 8 of their last 10 away games before Game 3. If Carolina delivers a strong bounce-back performance, a multi-goal victory is certainly within their capabilities.

The Washington Nationals are a strong road team, holding an impressive 22-13 record away from home this season. Their offense is performing exceptionally well, ranking first in runs scored, fifth in OPS, and sixth in Weighted Runs Created Plus (wRC+) across MLB. They face Giants starter Adrian Houser, who carries a high 5.49 ERA and struggled in his last outing, allowing four runs in just 3.2 innings. Furthermore, significant line movement has favored Washington, with their moneyline shifting from +104 to -106, indicating sharp money backing the Nationals despite initial public sentiment for the Giants.

The Texas Rangers have demonstrated clear dominance over the Kansas City Royals this season, winning all three head-to-head matchups and outscoring them 22-11. Texas enters this game in good form, having won seven of their last nine contests. The Royals' offensive potential is significantly hampered by the questionable availability of key players like Witt, Perez, and Caglianone, and their pitching staff faces uncertainty with Stephen Kolek on bereavement. Reverse line movement, where the Rangers' moneyline moved from -118 to -122 despite heavy public betting on Kansas City, signals sharp money supporting Texas.

The Philadelphia Phillies are in excellent recent form, boasting a 7-3 record over their last 10 games. They send Cristopher Sanchez to the mound, who is having a standout season with a 1.54 ERA and 113 strikeouts through 93.1 innings, ranking second among qualified MLB pitchers in ERA. This strong pitching advantage, coupled with the Phillies' consistent recent performance, provides a significant edge for them to secure a multi-run victory against the 32-35 Blue Jays. Kyle Schwarber is also a hot bat for the Phillies, slashing .357/.438/.595 with three homers in his last 11 games.

The Milwaukee Brewers are currently in strong form, holding a 7-3 record over their last 10 games. They are coming off an impressive 15-14 victory against the Athletics yesterday, showcasing their offensive firepower. While the Athletics' starter J. Ginn has a respectable 2.74 ERA, the Brewers' overall momentum and offensive capabilities make them a favorable pick at nearly even odds. Key hitter Jake Bauers has been exceptional for Milwaukee, slashing .286/.381/.531 with 12 homers and 43 RBI this season.

Despite previous high-scoring head-to-head games, the Kansas City Royals' offense is facing significant uncertainty due to potential injuries to key players such as Witt, Perez, and Caglianone. While Nathan Eovaldi has been inconsistent for the Rangers, Royals starter Stephen Kolek has demonstrated effectiveness in preventing runs at home. With both teams having a rest day on Monday, the bullpens should be fresh, further supporting a lower-scoring outcome. The Under 9.5 at plus money presents value, especially considering the Royals' potential offensive limitations.
